concerns.text: T020-T026 (US2) are marked as independent of US1, but T020/T021 explicitly consume the 'generated traces' produced by T012-T017 (US1). The plan states 'User stories can then proceed in parallel', but US2 cannot execute without the data artifacts from US1. This violates the 'Producer before consumer' dependency rule.

T029-T037 (US3) depend on the 'global rule set' and 'per-trace compressibility scores' generated in US2 (T023-T026). The current ordering implies US3 can start after Foundational (Phase 2), but it strictly requires US2 completion. The 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section incorrectly lists US3 as only depending on Foundational.

T010 and T011 are marked [P] (parallel-safe) and placed before T012 (implementation). However, T010 is a 'Contract test for generated trace schema' which requires the schema (T004) to exist, but T011 is an 'Integration test for dataset generation pipeline' which requires the implementation (T012) to exist to fail. Placing integration tests before the code they test violates the 'Tests MUST be written and FAIL before implementation' logic if they are meant to run immediately, or creates a circular dependency if they are meant to run after. They should be grouped with implementation or explicitly sequenced after T012.

T024 mentions calculating 'Compressibility Score' conditioned on 'Fidelity >= 90%'. This fidelity metric is derived from the benchmarking in US3 (T031-T033). US2 cannot calculate this score without the results of US3, creating a logical circular dependency in the task flow. The score definition needs to be decoupled or the task order adjusted to reflect that this specific metric is a post-US3 calculation.
